clean_text: Raspberry Mojito Cocktail Recipe | How to make a Raspberry Mojito | BACARDÍ Global OUR RUMS OUR RUMS White Rum BACARDÍ CARTA BLANCA Flavors Flavors Range Spiced BACARDÍ Spiced Dark Rum BACARDÍ Carta Oro BACARDÍ Carta Negra BACARDÍ Añejo Cuatro BACARDÍ Reserva Ocho BACARDÍ Gran Reserva Diez BACARDÍ Gran Reserva Limitada ready to enjoy BACARDÍ x coca-cola Ready to Drink cocktails Ready to Serve cocktails BACARDÍ Coquito View all our rums Cocktails Experiences Experiences Casa BACARDÍ Puerto Rico Visit the World’s largest premium rum distillery. SUSTAINABILITY Our World FAQS BASE RUM BACARDÍ Raspberry rum COCKTAILS Raspberry Mojito Mojitos come in all shapes and sizes. A combination of raspberries, mint, and BACARDÍ Raspberry rum, this one lends a berry kick to the traditional mojito. VIDEO HOW TO MAKE A RASPBERRY MOJITO A raspberry twist on the classic mojito for a kick of fruity flavour. How to make a Raspberry Mojito 50 ml BACARDÍ Raspberry rum 3 raspberries 4 lime wedges 2 tsp sugar 12 mint leaves top with club soda Servings Copy Raspberry Mojito ingredients Copied! Raspberry Mojito Recipe One step at a time 1 Build Build all ingredients in a highball glass and lightly muddle. 2 Stir Add crushed ice and stir. 3 Top Top with club soda and stir lightly before serving. 4 Garnish Garnish with a sprig of fresh mint and a raspberry. MERCHANDISE GET KITTED OUT Glasses. Squeezers. Cocktail kits. Discover everything you need to create standout BACARDÍ cocktails. SHOP NOW OUR RECOMMENDED RUM COCKTAILS Like This? Try These If this is your kind of cocktail, here’s our selection of other BACARDÍ cocktails that might suit your taste View all Rum cocktails Mojito Raspberry & Sonic Lime & Soda Coconut Mismo Skinny Mojito Drag to discover more View all Rum cocktails FAQ WANT TO KNOW MORE? How do you make rum? Rum is made using only three ingredients: molasses from sugarcane, yeast and water. Ingredients are fermented, then distilled, aged, filtered and blended. What is the difference between dark, gold and white rum? Rum is a wonderfully complex and diverse spirit. Color can give us clues about time spent aging or the flavours that might be found within. Dark can be bold, sweet (or dry) and even smoky, gold is rich and soothing, and white is smooth, subtle and often refreshing. But color can be misleading. The best way to truly understand a rum is to taste it for yourself. SOCIAL MOVE WITH US A soft sway, or a lively dance. We have something for everyone.
